extraction.one_line_summary: FERRON Valentin attacked inside final kilometer from six-rider breakaway, winning while more experienced riders BAGIOLI, BARGUIL, and ROLLAND hesitated and looked at each other
tactical_patterns: BAGIOLI Andrea's GC position (3:02 down) forced aggressive breakaway policing from INEOS/Jumbo, splitting peloton via pace not terrain.
Trek-Segafredo/Jumbo/INEOS collaborated to distance GROENEWEGEN Dylan via tempo on rolling roads, not just climbs—crosswind exploitation at 22kph.
Experienced breakaway riders (BAGIOLI/BARGUIL/ROLLAND) all marked each other final kilometer, allowing lesser-known FERRON to solo—reputation paralysis beats experience.

rider_signals.FERRON Valentin: positive: 24-year-old exploited hesitation from marquee breakaway names by attacking final kilometer when others marked each other
rider_signals.BAGIOLI Andrea: positive: Aggressive breakaway riding from 3:02 GC deficit forced multiple teams to chase hard enough to split peloton
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: negative: Former double Tour stage winner showed hesitation in six-man sprint finale, unable to respond to unknown rider's attack
rider_signals.ROLLAND Pierre: negative: KOM jersey holder marked in breakaway finale rather than initiating, beaten by less experienced rider's opportunism
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Extended high-tempo pulling for VAN AERT visible via salt/sweat on jersey—reliable domestique work across multiple stages
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Successfully distanced again by coordinated tempo from Trek/Jumbo/INEOS on non-mountainous terrain, tactical target confirmed
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Sat out breakaway sprint finish despite yellow jersey, conserving for later stages while GROENEWEGEN took peloton sprint
